comparisons.name: Vermont Senior Companion Program
comparisons.dataShapeNotes: Volunteer companion program (not direct client benefits); eligibility for companions only; income at ~200% FPL with annual updates; statewide via 4 regional agencies; no asset test or client application process.
gotchas: This is a volunteer program for low-income seniors (55+) to help other elderly; families do not 'qualify' or 'apply' directly for services—their loved one must be matched with a companion volunteer. Income eligibility uses approx. 200% poverty level (updates yearly). Stipend non-taxable and benefit-protected. Not a paid job or direct client service signup.[1][2]
Income examples from sources vary slightly by year; verify current with agency.
comparisons.officialSource: No single primary .gov URL; program via AmeriCorps Seniors, administered by Vermont Area Agencies on Aging (e.g., www.agewellvt.org).[1][2]
